You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: fc16aa0d).

## Issue to Resolve

Format parameters in lambda
After updating to 0.49.0 this is still not working as ktlint expects the formatting to look like this:

```kotlin
fun combine(a: Any, b: Any, c: (Any, Any) -> Unit) {
  c(a, b)
}

class Foo {
  fun foo() {
    combine(
      Any(),
      Any()
    ) { a,
              b -> // Unexpected indentation (8) (should be 14) (standard:indent)
      TODO()
    }
  }
}
```

_Originally posted by @eygraber in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756#issuecomment-1520584883_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

In `0.49.0` it works correctly for code style `ktlint_official`. With the other code styles the formatting is indeed incorrect.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 158→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 159→
 160→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 161→
 162→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 163→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 164→        indentConfig =
 165→            IndentConfig(
 166→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 167→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 168→            )
 169→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 170→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 171→        }
 172→    }
 173→
 174→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 175→        node: ASTNode,
 176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 178→    ) {
 179→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 180→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 181→            node
 182→                .nextLeaf()
 183→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 184→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 185→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 186→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 187→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 188→                    }
 189→                }
 190→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 191→        }
 192→
 193→        when {
 194→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 195→                line++
 196→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 197→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 198→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 199→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 200→                    )
 201→                }
 202→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 203→            }
 204→
 205→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 206→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 207→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 208→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 209→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 210→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 211→                startIndentContext(
 212→                    fromAstNode = node,
 213→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 214→                )
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 217→                visitValueArgument(node)
 218→
 219→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 220→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 221→
 222→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 223→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 224→                startIndentContext(node)
 225→
 226→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 227→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 230→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 231→                startIndentContext(node)
 232→
 233→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 234→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 235→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 236→                startIndentContext(
 237→                    fromAstNode = node,
 238→                    childIndent = "",
 239→                )
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == IF ->
 242→                visitIf(node)
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 245→                visitLbrace(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 248→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 249→                startIndentContext(
 250→                    fromAstNode = node,
 251→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 252→                )
 253→
 254→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 255→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 256→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 259→                visitValueParameter(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 262→                visitFun(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 265→                visitClass(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 268→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 269→
 270→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 271→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 272→
 273→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 274→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 275→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 276→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 277→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 278→                ) {
 279→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 280→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 281→                    //    .bar[0] {
 282→                    //        "foobar"
 283→                    //    }
 284→                    startIndentContext(
 285→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 286→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 287→                    )
 288→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 289→                    startIndentContext(node)
 290→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 291→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 292→                } else {
 293→                    startIndentContext(node)
 294→                }
 295→            }
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 298→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 299→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 302→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 303→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 304→
 305→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 306→                visitWhen(node)
 307→
 308→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 309→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 310→
 311→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 312→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 313→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 314→
 315→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 316→                visitKdoc(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 319→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 320→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 321→
 322→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 323→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 324→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 325→
 326→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 327→                visitLBracket(node)
 328→
 329→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 330→                visitNullableType(node)
 331→
 332→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 333→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 334→
 335→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 336→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 337→
 338→            else -> {
 339→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 340→            }
 341→        }
 342→    }
 343→
 344→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 345→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 346→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 347→            //     val foo = foo(
 348→            //         parameterName =
 349→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 350→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 351→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 352→            //     )
 353→            startIndentContext(
 354→                fromAstNode = node,
 355→                lastChildIndent = "",
 356→            )
 357→        }
 358→    }
 359→
 360→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 361→        node
 362→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 363→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 364→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 365→                val nextToAstNode =
 366→                    startIndentContext(
 367→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 368→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→
 370→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 371→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 372→                    startIndentContext(
 373→                        fromAstNode = node,
 374→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 375→                        childIndent = "",
 376→                    )
 377→                }
 378→            }
 379→    }
 380→
 381→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 382→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 383→        node
 384→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 385→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 386→                nextToAstNode =
 387→                    startIndentContext(
 388→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 389→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 390→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 391→            }
 392→
 393→        node
 394→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 395→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 396→            ?.nextLeaf()
 397→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 398→                nextToAstNode =
 399→                    startIndentContext(
 400→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 401→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                        childIndent = "",
 403→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 404→            }
 405→        node
 406→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 407→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 408→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 409→                nextToAstNode =
 410→                    startIndentContext(
 411→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 412→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 413→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 414→            }
 415→        startIndentContext(
 416→            fromAstNode = node,
 417→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 418→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 419→        )
 420→    }
 421→
 422→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 423→        // Outer indent context
 424→        val rbrace =
 425→            requireNotNull(
 426→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 427→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 428→        startIndentContext(
 429→            fromAstNode = node,
 430→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 431→            firstChildIndent = "",
 432→            lastChildIndent = "",
 433→        )
 434→
 435→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 436→        node
 437→            .treeParent
 438→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 439→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 440→            ?.let { arrow ->
 441→                startIndentContext(
 442→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 443→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 444→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 445→                )
 446→                startIndentContext(
 447→                    fromAstNode = node,
 448→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 449→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 450→                )
 451→            }
 452→    }
 453→
 454→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 455→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 456→            // val fieldExample =
 457→            //      LongNameClass {
 458→            //              paramA,
 459→            //              paramB,
 460→            //              paramC ->
 461→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 462→            //      }
 463→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 464→        } else {
 465→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 466→            // val fieldExample =
 467→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 468→            //                     paramB,
 469→            //                     paramC ->
 470→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 471→            //     }
 472→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 473→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 474→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 475→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 476→                            " { "
 477→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 478→                }
 479→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 480→        }
 481→
 482→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 483→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 484→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 485→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 486→
 487→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 488→        startIndentContext(
 489→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 490→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 491→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 492→            childIndent = "",
 493→        )
 494→    }
 495→
 496→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 497→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 498→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 499→        node
 500→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 501→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 502→                nextToAstNode =
 503→                    startIndentContext(
 504→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 505→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 506→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 507→            }
 508→
 509→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 510→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 511→            //     fun process(
 512→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 513→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 514→            //     ): List<Output>
 515→            node
 516→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 517→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 518→                    nextToAstNode =
 519→                        startIndentContext(
 520→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 521→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 522→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 523→                }
 524→        }
 525→
 526→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 527→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 528→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 529→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 530→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 531→        ) {
 532→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 533→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 534→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 535→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 536→        } else {
 537→            startIndentContext(
 538→                fromAstNode = node,
 539→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 540→                childIndent = "",
 541→            )
 542→        }
 543→    }
 544→
 545→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 546→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 547→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 548→        val eqOrBlock =
 549→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 550→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 551→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 552→            nextToAstNode =
 553→                startIndentContext(
 554→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 555→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 556→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 557→        }
 558→
 559→        node
 560→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 561→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 562→                nextToAstNode =
 563→                    startIndentContext(
 564→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 565→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 566→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 567→            }
 568→
 569→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 570→        startIndentContext(
 571→            fromAstNode = node,
 572→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 573→            childIndent = "",
 574→        )
 575→    }
 576→
 577→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 578→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 579→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 580→        node
 581→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 582→            ?.let { where ->
 583→                val typeConstraintList =
 584→                    requireNotNull(
 585→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 586→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 587→                require(
 588→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 589→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 590→                nextToAstNode =
 591→                    startIndentContext(
 592→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 593→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 594→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 595→            }
 596→
 597→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 598→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 599→            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 600→            nextToAstNode =
 601→                startIndentContext(
 602→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 603→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 604→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 605→        } else {
 606→            node
 607→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 608→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 609→                    nextToAstNode =
 610→                        startIndentContext(
 611→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 612→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 613→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 614→                }
 615→        }
 616→
 617→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 618→        startIndentContext(
 619→            fromAstNode = node,
 620→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 621→            childIndent = "",
 622→        )
 623→    }
 624→
 625→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 626→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 627→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 628→        node
 629→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 630→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 631→                nextToAstNode =
 632→                    startIndentContext(
 633→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 634→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 635→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 636→            }
 637→
 638→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 639→        startIndentContext(
 640→            fromAstNode = node,
 641→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 642→            childIndent = "",
 643→        )
 644→    }

 646→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 647→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 648→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 649→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 650→            val conditionIndentContext =
 651→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 652→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 653→            startIndentContext(
 654→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 655→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 656→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 657→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 658→            )
 659→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 660→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 661→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 662→        ) {
 663→            startIndentContext(node)
 664→        }
 665→    }
 666→
 667→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 668→        startIndentContext(
 669→            fromAstNode = node,
 670→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 671→        )
 672→    }

 674→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 675→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 676→        node
 677→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 678→            ?.let { lpar ->
 679→                nextToASTNode =
 680→                    startIndentContext(
 681→                        fromAstNode = lpar,
 682→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 683→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 684→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 685→            }
 686→        startIndentContext(
 687→            fromAstNode = node,
 688→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 689→        )
 690→    }
 691→
 692→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 693→        node
 694→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 695→            ?.let { arrow ->
 696→                val outerIndent =
 697→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 698→                        ""
 699→                    } else {
 700→                        indentConfig.indent
 701→                    }
 702→                startIndentContext(
 703→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 704→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 705→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 706→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 707→                )
 708→                startIndentContext(
 709→                    fromAstNode = node,
 710→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 711→                    childIndent = "",
 712→                )
 713→            }
 714→    }

 716→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {
 717→        startIndentContext(
 718→            fromAstNode = node,
 719→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 720→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 721→        )
 722→    }
 723→
 724→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 725→        node
 726→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 727→            ?.nextLeaf()
 728→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 729→                startIndentContext(
 730→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 731→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 732→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 733→                )
 734→            }
 735→    }
 736→
 737→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 738→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 739→        node
 740→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 741→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 742→                nextToASTNode =
 743→                    startIndentContext(
 744→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 745→                        toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 746→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 747→            }
 748→        // No indent on preceding annotations and comments
 749→        startIndentContext(
 750→            fromAstNode = node,
 751→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 752→            childIndent = "",
 753→        )
 754→    }

 756→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 757→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 758→        node
 759→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 760→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 761→            ?.let { rpar ->
 762→                startIndentContext(
 763→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 764→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 765→                )
 766→            }
 767→        node
 768→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 769→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 770→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 771→            }
 772→    }
 773→
 774→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 775→        node
 776→            .treeParent
 777→            .takeUnless {
 778→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 779→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 780→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 781→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 782→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 783→                startIndentContext(
 784→                    fromAstNode = node,
 785→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 786→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 787→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 788→                )
 789→            }
 790→    }

 792→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {
 793→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 794→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 795→        node
 796→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 797→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 798→                nextToAstNode =
 799→                    startIndentContext(
 800→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 801→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 802→                        childIndent = "",
 803→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 804→            }
 805→        startIndentContext(
 806→            fromAstNode = node,
 807→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 808→        )
 809→    }

 811→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 812→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 813→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 814→        node
 815→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 816→            ?.let { eq ->
 817→                nextToAstNode =
 818→                    startIndentContext(
 819→                        fromAstNode = eq,
 820→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 821→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 822→            }
 823→
 824→        node
 825→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 826→            ?.let {
 827→                startIndentContext(
 828→                    fromAstNode = node,
 829→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 830→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 831→                )
 832→            }
 833→    }
 834→
 835→    private fun visitTryCatchFinally(node: ASTNode) {
 836→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 837→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 838→        node
 839→            .findChildByType(FINALLY)
 840→            ?.let { finally ->
 841→                nextToAstNode =
 842→                    startIndentContext(
 843→                        fromAstNode = finally,
 844→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 845→                        childIndent = "",
 846→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 847→            }
 848→
 849→        node
 850→            .findChildByType(CATCH)
 851→            ?.let { catch ->
 852→                nextToAstNode =
 853→                    startIndentContext(
 854→                        fromAstNode = catch,
 855→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 856→                        childIndent = "",
 857→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 858→            }
 859→
 860→        startIndentContext(
 861→            fromAstNode = node,
 862→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 863→            lastChildIndent = "",
 864→        )
 865→    }
 866→
 867→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 868→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 869→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 870→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 871→                modifierList
 872→                    .children()
 873→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 874→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 875→            }
 876→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 877→            ?: this
 878→    }
 879→
 880→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 881→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 882→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 883→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 884→        ) {
 885→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 886→        }
 887→        return fromAstNode!!
 888→    }

 890→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 891→        node
 892→            .parents()
 893→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 894→            .lastOrNull()
 895→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 896→
 897→    private fun currentIndent() =
 898→        indentContextStack
 899→            .peekLast()
 900→            .indent()
 901→
 902→    private fun startIndentContext(
 903→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 904→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 905→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 906→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 907→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 908→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 909→    ): IndentContext =
 910→        IndentContext(
 911→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 912→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 913→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 914→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 915→            childIndent = childIndent,
 916→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 917→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 918→            LOGGER.trace {
 919→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 920→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 921→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 922→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 923→            }
 924→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 925→        }
 926→
 927→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 928→        node: ASTNode,
 929→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 930→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 931→    ) {
 932→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 933→            LOGGER.trace {
 934→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 935→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 936→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 937→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 938→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 939→            }
 940→            indentContextStack
 941→                .removeLast()
 942→                .also {
 943→                    LOGGER.trace {
 944→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 945→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 946→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 947→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 948→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 949→                    }
 950→                }
 951→        }
 952→    }
 953→
 954→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 955→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 956→            indentContextStack
 957→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 958→        }
 959→    }
 960→
 961→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 962→        node: ASTNode,
 963→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 964→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 965→    ) {
 966→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 967→            return
 968→        }
 969→
 970→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 971→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 972→        val text = node.text
 973→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 974→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 975→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 976→                emit(
 977→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 978→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 979→                    true,
 980→                )
 981→            } else {
 982→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 983→                // been emitted.
 984→            }
 985→            LOGGER.trace {
 986→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 987→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 988→            }
 989→            if (autoCorrect) {
 990→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 991→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 992→                )
 993→            }
 994→        } else {
 995→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 996→        }
 997→    }
 998→
 999→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1000→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1001→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1002→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1003→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1004→        }
1005→
1006→        nextLeaf
1007→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1008→            ?.let { comment ->
1009→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1010→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1011→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1012→                }
1013→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1014→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1015→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1016→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1017→                    return true
1018→                }
1019→            }
1020→        return false
1021→    }
1022→
1023→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1024→
1025→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
1026→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
1027→
1028→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1029→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1030→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1031→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1032→            when {
1033→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1034→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1035→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1036→
1037→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1038→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1039→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1040→
1041→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1042→            }
1043→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1044→    }
1045→
1046→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1047→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1048→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1049→            SPACE -> {
1050→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1051→                    emit(
1052→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1053→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1054→                        true,
1055→                    )
1056→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1057→                } else {
1058→                    nodeIndent
1059→                }
1060→            }
1061→
1062→            TAB -> {
1063→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1064→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1065→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1066→                    emit(
1067→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1068→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1069→                        true,
1070→                    )
1071→                    indentConfig
1072→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1074→                } else {
1075→                    nodeIndent
1076→                }
1077→            }
1078→        }
1079→    }
1080→
1081→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1082→        node: ASTNode,
1083→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1084→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1085→    ) {
1086→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1087→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)
1088→        }
1089→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1090→    }
1091→
1092→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
1093→        this != null &&
1094→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
1095→            firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
1096→
1097→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1098→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1099→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1100→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1101→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1102→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1103→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1104→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1105→                }
1106→
1107→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1108→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1109→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1110→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1111→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1112→                }
1113→
1114→                else -> ""
1115→            }
1116→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1117→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1118→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1119→        } else {
1120→            ""
1121→        }
1122→    }
1123→
1124→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1125→        IndentContext(
1126→            fromASTNode = node,
1127→            nodeIndent = "",
1128→            firstChildIndent = "",
1129→            childIndent = "",
1130→            lastChildIndent = "",
1131→            activated = true,
1132→        )
1133→
1134→    private companion object {
1135→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1136→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1137→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1138→    }
1139→
1140→    private data class IndentContext(
1141→        /**
1142→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1143→         */
1144→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1145→        /**
1146→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1147→         * on which the indent context starts.
1148→         */
1149→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1150→        /**
1151→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1152→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1153→         */
1154→        val nodeIndent: String,
1155→        /**
1156→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1157→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1158→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1159→         */
1160→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1161→        /**
1162→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1163→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1164→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1165→         */
1166→        val childIndent: String,
1167→        /**
1168→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1169→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1170→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1171→         */
1172→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1173→        /**
1174→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1175→         */
1176→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1177→    ) {
1178→        val nodes: String
1179→            get() =
1180→                fromASTNode
1181→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1182→                    ?.leaves()
1183→                    .orEmpty()
1184→                    .takeWhile {
1185→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1186→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1187→                    }
1188→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1189→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1190→
1191→        fun indent() =
1192→            if (activated) {
1193→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1194→            } else {
1195→                nodeIndent
1196→            }
1197→
1198→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1199→    }
1200→}
1201→
1202→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1203→
1204→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1205→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1206→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1207→            Pair("[", "]")
1208→        } else {
1209→            Pair("", "")
1210→        }
1211→    return prefix
1212→        .plus(
1213→            this
1214→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1215→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1216→        ).plus(suffix)
1217→}
1218→
1219→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1220→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1221→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1222→) {
1223→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1224→        expectedIndent: String,
1225→        node: ASTNode,
1226→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1227→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1228→    ) {
1229→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1230→        node
1231→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1232→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1233→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1234→            ?.let { _ ->
1235→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1236→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1237→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1238→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1239→                    emit(
1240→                        node.startOffset,
1241→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1242→                        false,
1243→                    )
1244→                    return
1245→                }
1246→
1247→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1248→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1249→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1250→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1251→                        // Allow:
1252→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1253→                        //       return """
1254→                        //           some text
1255→                        //           """.trimIndent
1256→                        //   }
1257→                        node
1258→                            .indent(false)
1259→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1260→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1261→                        // Allow:
1262→                        //   fun foo(
1263→                        //       bar: String
1264→                        //   ) = """
1265→                        //       $bar
1266→                        //       """.trimIndent
1267→                        node
1268→                            .indent(false)
1269→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1270→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1271→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1272→                        // should have no indent as well.
1273→                        ""
1274→                    } else {
1275→                        expectedIndent
1276→                    }
1277→                node.children()
1278→                    .forEach {
1279→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1280→                            (
1281→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1282→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1283→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1284→                                )
1285→                        ) {
1286→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1287→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1288→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1289→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1290→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1291→                                } else {
1292→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1293→                                }
1294→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1295→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1296→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1297→                                emit(
1298→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1299→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1300→                                        "string",
1301→                                    true,
1302→                                )
1303→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1304→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1305→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1306→                                    )
1307→                                }
1308→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1309→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1310→                                // the closing quotes.
1311→                                emit(
1312→                                    it.startOffset,
1313→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1314→                                    true,
1315→                                )
1316→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1317→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1318→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1319→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1320→                                        )
1321→                                    } else {
1322→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1323→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1324→                                        )
1325→                                    }
1326→                                }
1327→                            }
1328→                        }
1329→                    }
1330→            }
1331→    }
1332→
1333→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1334→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1335→            FUN ==
1336→            prevCodeLeaf()
1337→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1338→                ?.treeParent
1339→                ?.elementType
1340→
1341→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType
1342→
1343→    /**
1344→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1345→     * the closing quotes.
1346→     */
1347→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1348→        children()
1349→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1350→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1351→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1352→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1353→            .let { indents ->
1354→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1355→                    indents
1356→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1357→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1358→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1359→                } else {
1360→                    indents
1361→                }
1362→            }
1363→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1364→            .minOrNull()
1365→            ?: 0
1366→
1367→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1368→
1369→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1370→
1371→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1372→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1373→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1374→
1375→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1376→        for (child in node.children()) {
1377→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1378→                val v = child.text
1379→                if (v == "\n") {
1380→                    return true
1381→                }
1382→            }
1383→        }
1384→        return false
1385→    }
1386→
1387→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1388→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1389→        val nonBlankLines =
1390→            this
1391→                .text
1392→                .split("\n")
1393→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1394→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1395→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1396→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1397→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1398→            nonBlankLines
1399→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1400→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1401→                }
1402→                .toCharArray()
1403→                .distinct()
1404→                .count()
1405→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1406→    }
1407→
1408→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1409→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1410→
1411→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1412→
1413→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1414→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1415→
1416→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1417→
1418→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1419→
1420→    /**
1421→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1422→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1423→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1424→     * split index.
1425→     */
1426→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1427→        assert(index >= 0)
1428→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1429→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1430→                if (it == -1) {
1431→                    this.length
1432→                } else {
1433→                    it
1434→                }
1435→            }
1436→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1437→        return Pair(
1438→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1439→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1440→        )
1441→    }
1442→
1443→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1444→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1445→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1446→            this
1447→        } else {
1448→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1449→        }
1450→    }
1451→
1452→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1453→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1454→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1455→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1456→        }
1457→        return node != this
1458→    }
1459→
1460→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1461→}
1462→
1463→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId
1464→

**Analyzing lambda parameter indentation issue****Investigating auto-correction failure in indentation**

I'm exploring why auto-correct doesn't adjust lambda parameter indentation as expected—while the indentation rule expects 14 spaces, the auto-correct seems stuck at 8, possibly due to interplay with ParameterListWrapping rule or how indent contexts are handled for the default style. I'm considering if conflicting formatting rules or missed triggers cause the issue and thinking about running tests to confirm.

30→     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions

32→     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
33→     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
34→     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
35→     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
36→     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
37→     * are still not fixed.
38→     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 14→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 15→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 16→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 17→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 18→* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 19→
 20→### Changed
 21→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 22→
 23→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 24→
 25→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 26→
 27→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 28→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 29→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 30→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 31→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 32→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 33→
 34→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 35→
 36→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 37→
 38→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 39→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 40→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 41→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 42→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 43→
 44→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 45→
 46→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 47→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 48→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 49→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 50→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 51→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 52→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 53→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 54→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 55→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 56→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 57→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 58→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 59→
 60→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 61→
 62→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 63→
 64→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 65→
 66→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 67→
 68→#### Experimental rules
 69→
 70→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 71→
 72→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 73→
 74→#### EditorConfig
 75→
 76→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 77→
 78→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 79→
 80→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 81→
 82→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 83→
 84→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 85→
 86→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 87→
 88→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 89→
 90→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 91→
 92→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 93→
 94→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 95→
 96→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
