197→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
198→
199→Custom rule sets build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
200→
201→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The type of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
202→
203→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
204→
205→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
206→
207→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
208→
209→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
210→
211→```kotlin
212→public class SomeRule : Rule(
213→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
214→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
215→) {
216→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
217→
218→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
219→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
220→  }
221→  
222→  ...
223→}
224→```
225→
226→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
227→
228→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
229→
230→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
231→
232→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
233→
234→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
235→
236→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
237→
238→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
239→
240→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere to the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
241→
242→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule. The about information of a rule is printed whenever a rule throws an exception which is caught by the Ktlint Rule Engine.
243→
244→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
245→
246→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
247→
248→```kotlin
249→public class SomeRule : Rule(
250→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
251→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
252→) {
253→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
254→
255→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
256→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
257→  }
258→  
259→  ...
260→}
261→```
262→
263→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
264→
265→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
266→
267→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
268→
269→Previously, the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
270→
271→#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
272→
273→Constant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` has been removed. The file path is passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
274→```kotlin
275→if (node.isRoot()) {
276→  val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
277→    ...
278→}
279→```
280→
281→### Added
282→
283→* Add new code style `ktlint_offical`. The code style is work in progress and should be considered a preview. It is intended to become the default code style in the next release. Please try it out and give your feedback. See [code styles](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/code-styles/) for more information. The following rules have been added to the `ktlint_official` code style (the rules can also be run for other code styles when enabled explicitly):
284→  * Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line.
285→  * Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing`. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a single if, if-else or if-else-if statement.
286→  * Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments`. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments (see [examples](See https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/experimental/#disallow-consecutive-comments)).
287→  * Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing`. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles, but then it needs to be enabled explicitly.
288→  * Add new experimental rule `no-blank-line-in-list`. This rule disallows blank lines to be used in super type lists, type argument lists, type constraint lists, type parameter lists, value argument lists, and value parameter lists. ([#1224](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1224))
289→  * Add new experimental rule `multiline-expression-wrapping`. This forces a multiline expression as value in an assignment to start on a separate line. ([#1217](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1217))
290→  * Add new experimental rule `string-template-indent`. This forces multiline string templates which are post-fixed with `.trimIndent()` to be formatted consistently. The opening and closing `"""` are placed on separate lines and the indentation of the content of the template is aligned with the `"""`. ([#925](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/925))
291→  * Add new experimental rule `if-else-wrapping`. This enforces that a single line if-statement is kept simple. A single line if-statement may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches a single line if-statement may not be wrapped in a block. ([#812](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/812))
292→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
293→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
294→* Recognize Kotlin Script when linting and formatting code from `stdin` with KtLint CLI ([#1832](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1832))
295→* Support Bill of Materials (BOM), now you can integrate Ktlint in your `build.gradle` like:
296→  ```kotlin
297→  dependencies {
298→      implementation(platform("com.pinterest:ktlint-bom:0.49.0"))
299→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-core")
300→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-reporter-html")
301→      implementation("com.pinterest:ktlint-ruleset-standard")
302→      ...
303→  }
304→  ```
305→* Add new experimental rule `enum-wrapping` for all code styles. An enum should either be a single line, or each enum entry should be defined on a separate line. ([#1903](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1903))
306→
307→### Removed
308→
309→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
310→* Remove CLI option `--print-ast`. Use IntelliJ IDEA PsiViewer plugin instead. ([#1925](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1925))
311→
312→### Fixed
313→
314→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
315→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
316→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
317→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
318→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
319→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
320→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
321→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
322→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
323→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))
324→* Wrap annotations on type arguments in same way as with other constructs `annotation`, `wrapping` ([#1725](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1725))
325→* Fix indentation of try-catch-finally when catch or finally starts on a newline `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))
326→* Fix indentation of a multiline typealias `indent` ([#1788](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1788))
327→* Fix false positive when multiple KDOCs exists between a declaration and another annotated declaration `spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations` ([#1802](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1802))
328→* Fix false positive when a single line statement containing a block having exactly the maximum line length is preceded by a blank line `wrapping` ([#1808](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1808))
329→* Fix false positive when a single line contains multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expressions `indent` ([#1830](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1830))
330→* Enforce spacing around rangeUntil operator `..<` similar to the range operator `..` in `range-spacing`  ([#1858](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1858))
331→* When `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout` contains a `|` but no import exists that match any pattern before the first `|` then do not report a violation nor insert a blank line `import-ordering` ([#1845](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1845))
332→* When negate-patterns only are specified in Ktlint CLI then automatically add the default include patterns (`**/*.kt` and `**/*.kts`) so that all Kotlin files excluding the files matching the negate-patterns will be processed ([#1847](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1847))
333→* Do not remove newlines from multiline type parameter lists `type-parameter-list-spacing` ([#1867](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1867))
334→* Wrap each type parameter in a multiline type parameter list `wrapping` ([#1867](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1867))
335→* Allow value arguments with a multiline expression to be indented on a separate line `indent` ([#1217](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1217))
336→* When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags (see [faq](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#are-formatter-tags-respected)) ([#670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/670)) 
337→* Remove trailing comma if last two enum entries are on the same line and trailing commas are not allowed. `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1905](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1905))
338→* Wrap annotated function parameters to a separate line in code style `ktlint_official` only. `function-signature`, `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1908](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1908))
339→* Wrap annotated projection types in type argument lists to a separate line `annotation` ([#1909](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1909))
340→* Add newline after adding trailing comma in parameter list of a function literal `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1911](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1911))
341→* Wrap annotations before class constructor in code style `ktlint_official`. `annotation` ([#1916](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1916))
342→* Annotations on type projections should be wrapped in same way as other annotations `annotation` ([#1917](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1917))
343→* An if-else followed by an elvis operator should not be wrapped in an else-block `multiline-if-else` ([#1904](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1904))
344→
345→### Changed
346→* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
347→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
348→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
349→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
350→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
351→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
352→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
353→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20` and Kotlin version to `1.8.20`.
354→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
355→* For the new code style `ktlint_official`, do not allow wildcard imports `java.util` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic` by default. Important: `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` needs to be set to value `unset` in order to enforce IntelliJ IDEA default formatter to not generate wildcard imports `no-wildcard-imports` ([#1797](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1797))
356→* Convert a single line block comment to an EOL comment if not preceded or followed by another code element on the same line `comment-wrapping` ([#1941](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1941))
357→* Ignore a block comment inside a single line block `comment-wrapping` ([#1942](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1942))
358→
359→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
360→
361→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
362→
363→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
364→
365→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
366→
367→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
368→
369→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
370→```editorconfig
371→[*.{kt,kts}]
372→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
373→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
374→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
375→```
376→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
377→
378→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
379→
380→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
381→```
382→KtLintRuleEngine(
383→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
384→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
385→        EditorConfig
386→            .builder()
387→            .section(
388→                Section
389→                    .builder()
390→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
391→                    .properties(
392→                        Property
393→                            .builder()
394→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
395→                            .value("enabled"),
396→                    ),
397→            )
398→            .build()
399→    )
400→)

content.thinking:
**Analyzing lambda parameter indentation nuances****Investigating indent calculation discrepancies**

I’m reasoning about how to correctly compute the expected indent for lambda parameters on the same line as `{`. The current method uses function name length, but it misses characters like closing parentheses before `{`, causing incorrect indent expectations. I think the fix should base the child indent on the first parameter’s actual column minus the base indent length, ensuring subsequent lines align properly. Figuring out how to extract and combine these indent lengths from AST nodes and indent contexts is tricky, but I’m considering adding debug logs or a focused test to verify the correct indent values and then adjust the calculation accordingly.**Analyzing indent context and logging strategies**

I'm working through how `nodeIndent` likely measures only whitespace indentation (excluding characters like `)`), which complicates calculations. Since it's hard to infer directly, I'm considering temporarily adding debug logs in indentation context setup and parameter indent calculation to record `nodeIndent`, `childIndent`, and relevant text positions. This should help clarify actual indent values during tests, guiding me to adjust the indent calculation to align with IntelliJ’s style, especially around trailing parentheses before lambdas.**Designing dynamic indent calculation**

I'm drafting code to compute `childIndent` based on the current base indent length and the first parameter’s column, aiming to adjust indentation properly for IntelliJ style. Because indent style could be spaces or tabs, I’ll need to use helper methods from `IndentConfig` to create an indent string matching the desired length and style rather than just spaces. This approach should make indentation more flexible and accurate.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→
  9→public data class IndentConfig(
 10→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 11→    /**
 12→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 13→     */
 14→    val tabWidth: Int,
 15→) {
 16→    /**
 17→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 18→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 19→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 20→     *    ```
 21→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 22→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 23→     *    ```
 24→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 25→     *    ```
 26→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 27→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 28→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 29→     *    )
 30→     *    ```
 31→     */
 32→    public constructor(
 33→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 34→        /**
 35→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 36→         */
 37→        tabWidth: Int,
 38→    ) : this(
 39→        indentStyle =
 40→            when (indentStyle) {
 41→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 43→            },
 44→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 45→    )
 46→
 47→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 48→
 49→    private val indentChar =
 50→        when (indentStyle) {
 51→            TAB -> '\t'
 52→            SPACE -> ' '
 53→        }
 54→
 55→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 56→        when (indentStyle) {
 57→            TAB -> ' '
 58→            SPACE -> '\t'
 59→        }
 60→
 61→    /**
 62→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 63→     * configuration.
 64→     */
 65→    public val disabled: Boolean
 66→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 67→
 68→    /**
 69→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 70→     * configuration.
 71→     */
 72→    public val indent: String =
 73→        if (disabled) {
 74→            ""
 75→        } else {
 76→            when (indentStyle) {
 77→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 78→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 79→            }
 80→        }
 81→
 82→    /**
 83→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
 84→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
 85→     *
 86→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
 87→     *
 88→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
 89→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
 90→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
 91→     */
 92→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
 93→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
 94→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
 95→        return when (indentStyle) {
 96→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
 97→            TAB -> {
 98→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
 99→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
100→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
101→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
102→            }
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
107→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
108→        val indent =
109→            if (index == -1) {
110→                text
111→            } else {
112→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
113→            }
114→        return indent
115→    }
116→
117→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
118→
119→    /**
120→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
121→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
122→     *
123→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
124→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
125→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
126→     */
127→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
128→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
129→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
130→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
131→    }
132→
133→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
134→
135→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
136→
137→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
138→        when (indentStyle) {
139→            SPACE -> "tab"
140→            TAB -> "space"
141→        }
142→
143→    public companion object {
144→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
145→
146→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
147→            IndentConfig(
148→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
149→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
150→            )
151→    }
152→}
153→

**Designing function parameter indentation logic****Evaluating edge cases and testing plans**

I’m thinking through tricky scenarios like lambdas with no spaces after `{` and multiline trailing lambdas assigned to variables, ensuring the indent logic always produces correct positive differences. I’m also planning thorough tests for IntelliJ and Android Studio code styles that confirm parameter alignment behaves as expected, including creating a dedicated test file to cover these cases.

 51→/**

 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(

104→     * silently overwritten.

111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"

118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)

120→        code

122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }

function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/CHANGELOG.md","old_string":"### Fixed
* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
","new_string":"### Fixed
* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
* Align multiline lambda parameters when using `intellij_idea` or `android_studio` code styles `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
"}

**Deciding on running lint diagnostics**

I'll follow the guidance about running the `read_lints` tool after editing files, so I plan to run it on both the modified IndentationRule.kt and the new test file to be safe, even though it might not produce any results if no new lint issues were introduced.

content.text:
Summary:
- Fixed the lambda-parameter indentation logic in `IndentationRule` by deriving the continuation indent from the actual position of the first parameter on the line, ensuring `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` styles align parameters with the first argument instead of the start of the call expression.
- Added regression coverage in the new `IndentationRuleLambdaParametersTest` to assert both code styles accept the aligned format and that misaligned parameters are auto-corrected.
- Documented the fix in `CHANGELOG.md` under the “Unreleased → Fixed” section, referencing issue #1756.

Tests:
- `./gradlew ktlint-ruleset-standard:test --tests com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.IndentationRuleLambdaParametersTest`

 103→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline

 105→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 106→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 107→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 108→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 109→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.parent

 111→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeSibling

 117→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 118→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement

 125→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 126→
 127→public class IndentationRule :
 128→    StandardRule(
 129→        id = "indent",
 130→        visitorModifiers =
 131→            setOf(
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 133→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 134→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 135→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 136→                ),
 137→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 138→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 139→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 140→                ),
 141→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 142→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 143→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 144→                ),
 145→            ),
 146→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 147→            setOf(
 148→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 149→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 150→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 151→            ),
 152→    ) {
 153→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 154→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 155→
 156→    private var line = 1
 157→
 158→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 159→
 160→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 161→
 162→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 163→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 164→        indentConfig =
 165→            IndentConfig(
 166→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 167→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 168→            )
 169→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 170→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 171→        }
 172→    }
 173→
 174→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 175→        node: ASTNode,
 176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 178→    ) {
 179→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 180→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 181→            node
 182→                .nextLeaf()
 183→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 184→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 185→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 186→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 187→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 188→                    }
 189→                }
 190→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 191→        }
 192→
 193→        when {
 194→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 195→                line++
 196→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 197→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 198→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 199→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 200→                    )
 201→                }
 202→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 203→            }
 204→
 205→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 206→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 207→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 208→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 209→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 210→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 211→                startIndentContext(
 212→                    fromAstNode = node,
 213→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 214→                )
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 217→                visitValueArgument(node)
 218→
 219→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 220→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 221→
 222→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 223→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 224→                startIndentContext(node)
 225→
 226→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 227→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 230→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 231→                startIndentContext(node)
 232→
 233→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 234→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 235→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 236→                startIndentContext(
 237→                    fromAstNode = node,
 238→                    childIndent = "",
 239→                )
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == IF ->
 242→                visitIf(node)
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 245→                visitLbrace(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 248→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 249→                startIndentContext(
 250→                    fromAstNode = node,
 251→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 252→                )
 253→
 254→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 255→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 256→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 259→                visitValueParameter(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 262→                visitFun(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 265→                visitClass(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 268→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 269→
 270→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 271→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 272→
 273→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 274→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 275→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 276→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 277→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 278→                ) {
 279→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 280→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 281→                    //    .bar[0] {
 282→                    //        "foobar"
 283→                    //    }
 284→                    startIndentContext(
 285→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 286→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 287→                    )
 288→                } else if (node.prevCodeSibling().isElvisOperator()) {
 289→                    startIndentContext(node)
 290→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 291→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 292→                } else {
 293→                    startIndentContext(node)
 294→                }
 295→            }
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 298→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 299→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 302→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 303→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 304→
 305→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 306→                visitWhen(node)
 307→
 308→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 309→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 310→
 311→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 312→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 313→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 314→
 315→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 316→                visitKdoc(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 319→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 320→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 321→
 322→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 323→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 324→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 325→
 326→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 327→                visitLBracket(node)
 328→
 329→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 330→                visitNullableType(node)
 331→
 332→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 333→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 334→
 335→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 336→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 337→
 338→            else -> {
 339→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 340→            }
 341→        }
 342→    }
 343→
 344→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 345→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 346→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 347→            //     val foo = foo(
 348→            //         parameterName =
 349→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 350→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 351→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 352→            //     )
 353→            startIndentContext(
 354→                fromAstNode = node,
 355→                lastChildIndent = "",
 356→            )
 357→        }
 358→    }
 359→
 360→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 361→        node
 362→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 363→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 364→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 365→                val nextToAstNode =
 366→                    startIndentContext(
 367→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 368→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→
 370→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 371→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 372→                    startIndentContext(
 373→                        fromAstNode = node,
 374→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 375→                        childIndent = "",
 376→                    )
 377→                }
 378→            }
 379→    }
 380→
 381→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 382→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 383→        node
 384→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 385→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 386→                nextToAstNode =
 387→                    startIndentContext(
 388→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 389→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 390→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 391→            }
 392→
 393→        node
 394→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 395→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 396→            ?.nextLeaf()
 397→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 398→                nextToAstNode =
 399→                    startIndentContext(
 400→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 401→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                        childIndent = "",
 403→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 404→            }
 405→        node
 406→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 407→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 408→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 409→                nextToAstNode =
 410→                    startIndentContext(
 411→                        fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 412→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 413→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 414→            }
 415→        startIndentContext(
 416→            fromAstNode = node,
 417→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 418→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 419→        )
 420→    }
 421→
 422→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 423→        // Outer indent context
 424→        val rbrace =
 425→            requireNotNull(
 426→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 427→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 428→        startIndentContext(
 429→            fromAstNode = node,
 430→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 431→            firstChildIndent = "",
 432→            lastChildIndent = "",
 433→        )
 434→
 435→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 436→        node
 437→            .treeParent
 438→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 439→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 440→            ?.let { arrow ->
 441→                startIndentContext(
 442→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 443→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 444→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 445→                )
 446→                startIndentContext(
 447→                    fromAstNode = node,
 448→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 449→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 450→                )
 451→            }
 452→    }
 453→
 454→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 455→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 456→            // val fieldExample =
 457→            //      LongNameClass {
 458→            //              paramA,
 459→            //              paramB,
 460→            //              paramC ->
 461→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 462→            //      }
 463→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 464→        } else {
 465→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 466→            // val fieldExample =
 467→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 468→            //                     paramB,
 469→            //                     paramC ->
 470→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 471→            //     }
 472→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 473→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 474→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 475→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 476→                            " { "
 477→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 478→                }
 479→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 480→        }
 481→
 482→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 483→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 484→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 485→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 486→
 487→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 488→        startIndentContext(
 489→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 490→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 491→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 492→            childIndent = "",
 493→        )
 494→    }
 495→
 496→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 497→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 498→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 499→        node
 500→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 501→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 502→                nextToAstNode =
 503→                    startIndentContext(
 504→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 505→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 506→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 507→            }
 508→
 509→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 510→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 511→            //     fun process(
 512→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 513→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 514→            //     ): List<Output>
 515→            node
 516→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 517→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 518→                    nextToAstNode =
 519→                        startIndentContext(
 520→                            fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 521→                            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 522→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 523→                }
 524→        }
 525→
 526→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 527→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 528→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 529→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 530→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 531→        ) {
 532→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 533→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 534→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 535→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 536→        } else {
 537→            startIndentContext(
 538→                fromAstNode = node,
 539→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 540→                childIndent = "",
 541→            )
 542→        }
 543→    }
 544→
 545→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 546→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 547→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 548→        val eqOrBlock =
 549→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 550→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 551→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 552→            nextToAstNode =
 553→                startIndentContext(
 554→                    fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 555→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 556→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 557→        }
 558→
 559→        node
 560→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 561→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 562→                nextToAstNode =
 563→                    startIndentContext(
 564→                        fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 565→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 566→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 567→            }
 568→
 569→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 570→        startIndentContext(
 571→            fromAstNode = node,
 572→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 573→            childIndent = "",
 574→        )
 575→    }
 576→
 577→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 578→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 579→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 580→        node
 581→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 582→            ?.let { where ->
 583→                val typeConstraintList =
 584→                    requireNotNull(
 585→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 586→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 587→                require(
 588→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 589→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 590→                nextToAstNode =
 591→                    startIndentContext(
 592→                        fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 593→                        toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 594→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 595→            }
 596→
 597→        val primaryConstructor = node.findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
 598→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && primaryConstructor != null) {
 599→            // Indent both constructor and super type list
 600→            nextToAstNode =
 601→                startIndentContext(
 602→                    fromAstNode = primaryConstructor.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 603→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 604→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 605→        } else {
 606→            node
 607→                .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 608→                ?.let { superTypeList ->
 609→                    nextToAstNode =
 610→                        startIndentContext(
 611→                            fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 612→                            toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 613→                        ).prevCodeLeaf()
 614→                }
 615→        }
 616→
 617→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 618→        startIndentContext(
 619→            fromAstNode = node,
 620→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 621→            childIndent = "",
 622→        )
 623→    }

 625→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 626→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 627→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 628→        node
 629→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 630→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 631→                nextToAstNode =
 632→                    startIndentContext(
 633→                        fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 634→                        toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 635→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 636→            }
 637→
 638→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 639→        startIndentContext(
 640→            fromAstNode = node,
 641→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 642→            childIndent = "",
 643→        )
 644→    }

 646→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 647→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 648→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 649→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 650→            val conditionIndentContext =
 651→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 652→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 653→            startIndentContext(
 654→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 655→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 656→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 657→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 658→            )
 659→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 660→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 661→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 662→        ) {
 663→            startIndentContext(node)
 664→        }
 665→    }

 667→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 668→        startIndentContext(
 669→            fromAstNode = node,
 670→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 671→        )
 672→    }

 677→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 678→            ?.let { lpar ->

 680→                    startIndentContext(

 682→                        toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 683→                        lastChildIndent = "",
 684→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 685→            }
 686→        startIndentContext(
 687→            fromAstNode = node,
 688→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 689→        )

 692→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 693→        node

 695→            ?.let { arrow ->
 696→                val outerIndent =
 697→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {

 700→                        indentConfig.indent

 702→                startIndentContext(
 703→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 704→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 705→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 706→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 707→                )
 708→                startIndentContext(
 709→                    fromAstNode = node,

 724→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {

 727→            ?.nextLeaf()

 745→                        toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 746→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()

 756→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {

 867→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 868→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 869→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 870→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 871→                modifierList
 872→                    .children()
 873→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 874→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 875→            }
 876→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 877→            ?: this
 878→    }
 879→
 880→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 881→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 882→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 883→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 884→        ) {
 885→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 886→        }
 887→        return fromAstNode!!
 888→    }
 889→
 890→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 891→        node
 892→            .parents()
 893→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 894→            .lastOrNull()
 895→            ?.elementType == CONDITION
 896→
 897→    private fun currentIndent() =
 898→        indentContextStack
 899→            .peekLast()
 900→            .indent()
 901→
 902→    private fun startIndentContext(
 903→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 904→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 905→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 906→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 907→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 908→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 909→    ): IndentContext =
 910→        IndentContext(
 911→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 912→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 913→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 914→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 915→            childIndent = childIndent,
 916→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 917→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 918→            LOGGER.trace {
 919→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 920→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 921→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 922→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 923→            }
 924→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 925→        }
 926→
 927→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 928→        node: ASTNode,
 929→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 930→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 931→    ) {
 932→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 933→            LOGGER.trace {
 934→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 935→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 936→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 937→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 938→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 939→            }
 940→            indentContextStack
 941→                .removeLast()
 942→                .also {
 943→                    LOGGER.trace {
 944→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 945→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 946→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 947→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 948→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 949→                    }
 950→                }
 951→        }
 952→    }
 953→
 954→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 955→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 956→            indentContextStack
 957→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 958→        }
 959→    }
 960→
 961→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 962→        node: ASTNode,
 963→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 964→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 965→    ) {
 966→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 967→            return
 968→        }
 969→
 970→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 971→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 972→        val text = node.text
 973→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 974→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 975→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 976→                emit(
 977→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 978→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 979→                    true,
 980→                )
 981→            } else {
 982→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 983→                // been emitted.
 984→            }
 985→            LOGGER.trace {
 986→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 987→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 988→            }
 989→            if (autoCorrect) {
 990→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 991→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 992→                )
 993→            }
 994→        } else {
 995→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 996→        }
 997→    }
 998→
 999→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
1000→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1001→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
1002→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1003→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
1004→        }
1005→
1006→        nextLeaf
1007→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
1008→            ?.let { comment ->
1009→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
1010→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1011→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
1012→                }
1013→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
1014→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
1015→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
1016→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
1017→                    return true
1018→                }
1019→            }
1020→        return false
1021→    }
1022→
1023→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
1024→
1025→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
1026→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
1027→
1028→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
1029→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
1030→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
1031→        val adjustedChildIndent =
1032→            when {
1033→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
1034→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1035→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1036→
1037→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1038→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1039→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1040→
1041→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1042→            }
1043→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1044→    }
1045→
1046→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1047→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1048→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1049→            SPACE -> {
1050→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1051→                    emit(
1052→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1053→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1054→                        true,
1055→                    )
1056→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1057→                } else {
1058→                    nodeIndent
1059→                }
1060→            }
1061→
1062→            TAB -> {
1063→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1064→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1065→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1066→                    emit(
1067→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1068→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1069→                        true,
1070→                    )
1071→                    indentConfig
1072→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1073→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1074→                } else {
1075→                    nodeIndent
1076→                }
1077→            }
1078→        }
1079→    }
1080→
1081→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1082→        node: ASTNode,
1083→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1084→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1085→    ) {
1086→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1087→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(codeStyle, indentConfig)
1088→        }
1089→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1090→    }
1091→
1092→    private fun ASTNode?.isElvisOperator() =
1093→        this != null &&
1094→            elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE &&
1095→            firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
1096→
1097→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1098→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1099→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1100→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1101→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1102→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1103→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1104→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1105→                }
1106→
1107→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1108→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1109→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1110→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1111→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1112→                }
1113→
1114→                else -> ""
1115→            }
1116→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1117→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1118→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1119→        } else {
1120→            ""
1121→        }
1122→    }
1123→
1124→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1125→        IndentContext(
1126→            fromASTNode = node,
1127→            nodeIndent = "",
1128→            firstChildIndent = "",
1129→            childIndent = "",
1130→            lastChildIndent = "",
1131→            activated = true,
1132→        )
1133→
1134→    private companion object {
1135→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1136→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1137→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1138→    }
1139→
1140→    private data class IndentContext(
1141→        /**
1142→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1143→         */
1144→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1145→        /**
1146→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1147→         * on which the indent context starts.
1148→         */
1149→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1150→        /**
1151→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1152→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1153→         */
1154→        val nodeIndent: String,
1155→        /**
1156→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1157→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1158→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1159→         */
1160→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1161→        /**
1162→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1163→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1164→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1165→         */
1166→        val childIndent: String,
1167→        /**
1168→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1169→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1170→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1171→         */
1172→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1173→        /**
1174→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1175→         */
1176→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1177→    ) {
1178→        val nodes: String
1179→            get() =
1180→                fromASTNode
1181→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1182→                    ?.leaves()
1183→                    .orEmpty()
1184→                    .takeWhile {
1185→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1186→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1187→                    }
1188→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1189→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1190→
1191→        fun indent() =
1192→            if (activated) {
1193→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1194→            } else {
1195→                nodeIndent
1196→            }
1197→
1198→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1199→    }
1200→}
1201→
1202→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1203→
1204→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1205→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1206→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1207→            Pair("[", "]")
1208→        } else {
1209→            Pair("", "")
1210→        }
1211→    return prefix
1212→        .plus(
1213→            this
1214→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1215→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1216→        ).plus(suffix)
1217→}
1218→
1219→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1220→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1221→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1222→) {
1223→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1224→        expectedIndent: String,
1225→        node: ASTNode,
1226→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

1341→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType

1343→    /**

1345→     * the closing quotes.
1346→     */
1347→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1348→        children()
1349→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1350→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1351→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1352→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }

1354→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1355→                    indents
1356→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1357→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1358→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1359→                } else {
1360→                    indents
1361→                }

1363→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1364→            .minOrNull()
1365→            ?: 0
